meta.name: Shiro Wat (Ethiopian Chickpea Stew)
meta.subtitle: Creamy, aromatic chickpea flour stew—weeknight-friendly and perfect with injera.

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Baseline shiro wat tuned for SSA catalogs with deterministic steps and optional heat controls.
Uses chickpea flour (shiro powder). If you have a spiced shiro mix, reduce berbere/salt accordingly.
Texture is adjustable from pourable to thick scoopable by changing simmer time and water.

summary: Shiro is a smooth Ethiopian stew made from chickpea flour simmered with onion, garlic, and berbere, often enriched with niter kibbeh or oil.

yield.notes: Serves 4–6 as a main with injera; 6–8 as part of a platter.

optional.label: Immersion blender (optional)
optional.notes: Not needed if you whisk well; can help if onions are chunky.

notes: Naturally gluten-free if chickpea flour is not cross-contaminated.
If using commercial shiro powder blends, check for additives and allergens.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (or niter kibbeh if not vegan)
notes: Traditional versions may use niter kibbeh (spiced clarified butter).
For vegan shiro, use neutral oil or olive oil.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion, finely diced (or grated)
notes: Finely diced helps the stew become smooth; grating speeds this up.
Onion quantity is key for authentic body and sweetness.

ingredients.label: Berbere spice blend
notes: Adjust to heat tolerance and whether your shiro powder is already seasoned.
For mild, start at 6 g; for hot, increase to 18 g.

ingredients.label: Paprika (optional, for color if berbere is mild)
Tomato paste
notes: Optional but common; adds sweetness and depth.
If using highly seasoned shiro powder, you can omit.

ingredients.label: Chickpea flour (shiro powder)
notes: This recipe assumes plain chickpea flour + spices.
If using a packaged shiro mix, reduce added spices and salt to taste.

ingredients.label: Water (or light vegetable broth)
notes: Start with 900–1000 ml; add more for a thinner shiro.
Shiro thickens as it simmers and again as it cools.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Seasoned shiro powders may already contain salt—add gradually and taste.

ingredients.label: Korarima (Ethiopian cardamom) or ground cardamom (pinch, optional)
ingredients.notes: A small amount adds a distinctive Ethiopian aroma.

ingredients.label: Kosseret (optional herb; substitute dried oregano in tiny pinch)
Green chili or jalapeño, sliced (optional garnish)

notes: Keep heat moderate and whisk frequently after adding flour to prevent lumps and sticking.
If shiro gets too thick, loosen with hot water 60–120 ml at a time.
Flavor improves after a short rest; reheat gently and loosen as needed.

changes.fat: Use 45 ml niter kibbeh instead of oil.
changes.finish: Stir in 1–2 tbsp niter kibbeh at the end for aroma.

changes.tomato: Increase tomato paste to 45 g and add 150 g crushed tomato; simmer 5 minutes before adding flour.

changes.spice: Use 6 g berbere, include paprika for color, and omit green chili garnish.

changes.onion: Dice onion slightly larger and cook longer; skip blending/extra whisking.

notes: Chickpea flour must be simmered to cook out raw flavor; keep at a gentle simmer for at least 10–12 minutes after thickening.
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate promptly.

fridge.notes: Store airtight. Reheat gently and loosen with water.

freezer.notes: Freeze in portions. Thaw in fridge overnight and reheat gently, whisking smooth.

steps.title: Sweat the onion
instructions: Heat oil (or niter kibbeh) in a medium pot over medium heat.
Add onion with a pinch of salt and cook, stirring frequently, until very soft and translucent (6–10 minutes).
If the pot gets dry, add 1–2 tbsp water and keep cooking—onion softness helps make shiro smooth.
checks: Onion is very soft, not browned heavily.

steps.title: Add aromatics and bloom spices
instructions: Stir in garlic (and ginger if using) and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
Add berbere (and paprika if using) and stir constantly for 30–45 seconds to bloom spices.
If using tomato paste, add it now and cook 1–2 minutes until it darkens slightly and smells sweet.
checks: Spices are evenly coated in fat and onion.

steps.title: Add water and bring to a simmer
instructions: Add about 750 ml of the water and whisk to loosen any browned bits.
checks: Liquid is gently simmering, not violently boiling.

steps.title: Whisk in chickpea flour (prevent lumps)
instructions: While whisking continuously, sprinkle chickpea flour into the simmering liquid in a steady stream.
Keep whisking until smooth—break up any lumps immediately.
checks: Mixture is smooth with no visible dry pockets or flour lumps.

steps.title: Simmer to cook and thicken
instructions: Simmer gently 12–15 minutes, stirring every 1–2 minutes (shiro can stick).
Adjust texture with remaining water: add 60–120 ml at a time to reach your preferred consistency.
Add salt gradually, tasting as you go (especially if using a seasoned shiro mix).
Optional: add a pinch of korarima/cardamom and/or kosseret near the end.
checks: Raw flour taste is gone; flavor is savory and aromatic.
Consistency is creamy (spoon-coating) and pourable/scoopable as desired.

steps.title: Rest and serve
instructions: Turn off heat and rest 2–3 minutes; whisk once more to smooth.
Serve hot with injera. Garnish with sliced green chili if desired.
checks: Shiro is smooth, glossy, and aromatic.
